directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: ARPU is defined as Average Revenue Per User. For which of the following companies ARPU in the month of January maximum for both the years combined?
explanation: ARPU = Revenue ÷ Users. Combined (both years): AirLet 2300/501 ≈ 4.6, Alliance 6600/642 ≈ 10.3, Dofaphone 4750/580 ≈ 8.2, Earcel 1000/88 ≈ 11.4, BainsNL 8050/862 ≈ 9.3. Earcel is the maximum. Hence (c).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: ARPU is defined as Average Revenue Per User. For how many companies, ARPU has increased in January 2011 over January 2010?
explanation: ARPU 2010 → 2011: AirLet 4.96 → 4.25 (↓), Alliance 11.11 → 9.43 (↓), Dofaphone 7.69 → 8.67 (↑), Earcel 9.52 → 13.04 (↑), BainsNL 9.00 → 9.66 (↑). It increased for 3 companies. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text:
In January 2011, VAS (Value Added Services) was launched by all these five telecom companies for the first time. The following table gives the percentage of revenue generated through VAS by these companies in the month of January 2011 (out of the total revenue generated in January 2011 as given in the bar chart):

AirLet — 18%, Alliance — 9%, Dofaphone — 12%, Earcel — 22%, BainsNL — 6%

Which of the following is NOT correct for the VAS revenue generated by these companies for the year 2011?

options.label: a
options.text: VAS revenue of Alliance is more than the VAS revenue of BainsNL.

options.label: b
options.text: VAS revenue of AirLet is more than the VAS revenue of Alliance.

options.label: c
options.text: If we arrange the VAS revenue of all these companies in ascending order, then VAS revenue of BainsNL will be the median.

explanation: The VAS percentages are given only for January 2011, but the question asks about the whole year 2011. With only one month’s data, the year’s VAS revenue cannot be determined. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text:
In January 2010, SMS Services was launched by all these five telecom companies for the first time. The following table gives the percentage of revenue generated through SMS services by these companies in the month of January 2010 (out of the total revenue generated in January 2010 as given in the bar chart):

AirLet — 12%, Alliance — 16%, Dofaphone — 9%, Earcel — 6%, BainsNL — 14%

What is the ratio of revenue generated through SMS services by Alliance Telecom to the revenue generated by Earcel through SMS in 2010?
explanation: The SMS percentages are given only for January 2010, but the question asks about the year 2010. With only one month’s data, the figures for the whole year cannot be determined. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For how many families, savings is non-negative for each of the years?
explanation: Savings = Income − Expense each year. Only the Agrawal family is non-negative in every year (0 in 2008, positive otherwise); every other family has at least one year with negative savings. So 1 family. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which of the following families’ total savings is maximum over the given period?
explanation: Only Verma and Agrawal have a positive total saving over the period, and Agrawal’s total exceeds Verma’s. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For how many families, total savings is negative over the given period?
explanation: Sharma, Sinha and Patel each have a negative total saving over the period — 3 families. Hence (b).
